# A typed TypeScript client, borrowed end to end

This directory is a proof, not a product: because Spock's GraphQL surface
speaks a specified, Hasura-mirrored dialect with standard introspection
(`docs/spec/graphql.md`), the existing GraphQL toolchain consumes it with
**zero Spock-specific tooling**. The types below are generated by plain
[`graphql-codegen`](https://the-guild.dev/graphql/codegen); any of its
siblings (gql.tada, urql, Apollo) would work the same way.

The schema is never written and never checked in — it is a pure function
of the contract, introspected from a running server.

## Run it

Start the server (from the repository root):

```sh
cargo run -p spock-cli -- run examples/instagram/v0.spock
```

Then, in this directory, use the repository's Node 24 LTS pin and install the
client tooling:

```sh
nvm use
npm install
npm run generate   # introspects http://127.0.0.1:4000/graphql/v1 → src/gql/
npm run check      # tsc --noEmit: the queries are statically typed
npm run demo       # runs src/main.ts against the live server
```

No server handy? The binary emits the same schema as a file
(`spock gen graphql-schema`, RFD 0010's offline bridge — the SDL comes
from the identical builder `spock run` serves, so it cannot drift). From
the repository root:

```sh
cargo run -p spock-cli -- gen graphql-schema examples/instagram/v0.spock \
  -o examples/instagram/client/schema.graphql
```

then, in this directory:

```sh
SPOCK_SCHEMA=schema.graphql npm run generate
```

(`schema.graphql` is a regenerable artifact; it stays untracked.)

The GraphQL operation types here are one half of the story; the other
half is contract-native: `spock gen types app.spock` emits row shapes,
insert/update shapes, and the **derived error codes as literal unions**
(`"user_username_taken" | …`) straight from the contract — the types the
future generic `spock` npm client will take (RFD 0010).

The demo reads the feed through nested reverse relations, inserts and
updates a row through the generated input types, and deliberately trips a
derived error to match on its code:

```
@maya: "first light" (2 likes)
@luis: "(untitled)" (1 likes)
@vera: no posts
created @nova at 2026-07-10T10:22:09.856742Z
updated bio: still typed
rejected as expected: user_username_taken on user.username
cleaned up — run me again
```

## What to look at

- `src/gql/graphql.ts` — generated, operation-driven: the result and
  variable types of exactly the operations `src/main.ts` declares, plus
  the input types those operations reference (`User_Insert_Input`,
  `User_Set_Input` — codegen's own casing of the dialect's
  `user_insert_input` / `user_set_input`). A type appears here when a
  document uses it. Regenerated by `npm run generate`; do not edit. (For
  schema-complete, contract-native types — every table, every error code
  — see `spock gen types`.)
- `src/main.ts` — the whole client: a ~25-line typed `fetch` executor plus
  operations. Misspell any field and `npm run check` fails.
- `user_username_taken` in the demo output — the derived error vocabulary
  (`docs/spec/v0.md` §6.1, frozen for v0.x) reaching TypeScript as data:
  clients match on codes, they never parse messages.

Point the codegen at a different server with
`SPOCK_URL=http://host:port/graphql/v1 npm run generate`. Edit the
`.spock` file, restart the server, regenerate — the client types follow
the schema, which follows the contract.
